predictions.text: OFAC will issue a General License (or equivalent written authorization/FAQ) that explicitly allows U.S. persons to purchase, finance, insure, or transport Iranian-origin crude oil for market-stabilization purposes.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolved YES if OFAC publishes a General License, FAQ, or other written authorization explicitly permitting (not merely waiving enforcement) U.S.-person transactions involving Iranian-origin crude oil (purchase/financing/insurance/transport), citing stabilization/price relief. Otherwise NO.
evidence: CNBC reports the U.S. is weighing releasing sanctioned Iranian crude to cool prices, implying possible sanctions-tool adjustments.
Heightened oil-price sensitivity amid Iran conflict increases incentive for targeted licensing tools.
monitoring_signals: OFAC General License postings and Iran-related FAQ updates
Treasury press releases and sanctions guidance changes
Reuters/Bloomberg reporting on sanctions carve-outs for oil flows
predictions.rationale: A stabilization-motivated carve-out is plausible but politically and legally sensitive; more likely to be narrow/conditional than broad.

predictions.text: The ECB will explicitly describe higher energy prices as an 'upside risk' (or clearly equivalent wording) to the inflation outlook in at least one monetary-policy statement, account, or press-conference transcript.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolved YES if ECB communications (press release, introductory statement, accounts of the monetary policy meeting, or Lagarde Q&A transcript) explicitly label energy prices as an upside risk to inflation (or materially similar phrase like 'adds upside risks to inflation'). Otherwise NO.
evidence: Reports of a 'perfect storm' in European bonds tied to war-driven inflation uncertainty suggest ECB will foreground energy risk.
Regional conflict risk is directly linked to energy supply and price volatility.
monitoring_signals: ECB meeting-day press releases and Lagarde transcripts
ECB accounts (minutes) language on inflation risks
Energy-price volatility and euro-area inflation surprise indices
predictions.rationale: ECB commonly frames energy as an inflation risk; current conflict-driven uncertainty makes explicit wording likely within the next few meetings.

predictions.text: U.S. Census Bureau New Home Sales (SAAR) will print at or below 550,000 in at least one monthly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolved YES if any New Residential Sales release (covering Feb–May 2026 sales, released by end of June) shows seasonally adjusted annual rate (SAAR) <= 550,000 (headline figure). Otherwise NO.
evidence: Recent report notes January new home sales plunged to the lowest since 2022, consistent with continued affordability/rate pressure.
monitoring_signals: Weekly mortgage-rate levels and purchase application trends
Homebuilder earnings commentary on cancellations and incentives
Monthly New Home Sales consensus vs actual surprises
predictions.rationale: Given recent weakness and high-rate environment, a sub-550k print is plausible, but monthly volatility and revisions keep odds near coin-flip.

predictions.text: The U.S. DOJ will announce at least one additional criminal case (complaint/indictment) alleging illegal export or diversion of advanced AI GPUs (e.g., Nvidia A/H-series or successor) to China via smuggling, shell companies, or false end-users.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolved YES if DOJ (or a U.S. Attorney’s Office) publicly releases an indictment/complaint naming defendants and alleging illegal export/diversion of advanced AI GPUs to China (including Hong Kong) beyond the already-reported Super Micro-related allegations. Otherwise NO.
evidence: CNBC reports prosecutors allege Super Micro employees diverted Nvidia chips to China, highlighting an enforcement push.
monitoring_signals: DOJ press releases on export-control and sanctions cases
BIS enforcement actions and entity-list additions
Seizure reports involving high-end GPUs and server shipments
predictions.rationale: Once one supply-chain is exposed, follow-on cases often emerge from shared intermediaries and logistics records.

predictions.text: OpenAI will ship a generally available (non-beta) desktop 'super app' update that includes an integrated web-browsing experience (browser-like tab/view inside the app) and a built-in coding workspace (editor/IDE-like interface), announced in OpenAI release notes or blog.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolved YES if OpenAI’s official release notes/blog (or app store changelog clearly attributable to OpenAI) states the desktop app now includes an in-app browsing interface and an integrated coding workspace/editor available to all users (or all paid users) as GA. Otherwise NO.
evidence: CNBC reports OpenAI plans a desktop 'super app' merging ChatGPT, browser, and coding tools, indicating near-term productization intent.
monitoring_signals: OpenAI desktop app release notes and product announcements
Mentions of embedded browser, tabs, or webviews in app updates
Codex/IDE feature expansions and desktop distribution changes
predictions.rationale: Feature integration is aligned with distribution strategy, but GA timing depends on reliability, safety review, and platform constraints.

predictions.text: A SEC Form D (or comparable filing) will be made for an AI-focused investment fund explicitly linked by major reporting to Jeff Bezos / his office / an affiliated entity (e.g., 'Project Prometheus'), and the same reporting will state a first close/committed capital of at least $5B.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolved YES if (1) a Form D (or equivalent) is filed for such a fund/vehicle and (2) at least two major outlets (WSJ/FT/Bloomberg/Reuters/NYT) report it is linked to Bezos (or his investment office/affiliated entity) and report first close/commitments >= $5B. Otherwise NO.
evidence: NYT reports Bezos is seeking a ~$100B AI-focused fund to modernize manufacturers, consistent with steps toward formal fundraising.
monitoring_signals: SEC Form D filings by Bezos-affiliated entities
Institutional LP disclosures and placement agent activity
Follow-on reporting from Bloomberg/Reuters/FT/WSJ
predictions.rationale: Mega-funds often leak early, but formal filing plus verified first-close magnitude is a higher bar, keeping probability modest.

predictions.text: Fervo (or a Fervo-led consortium) will announce a signed power purchase agreement (PPA) or capacity contract for geothermal electricity totaling at least 200 MW nameplate (or contracted capacity) for a single multi-year offtake.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolved YES if Fervo publicly announces (press release, blog, regulatory filing by counterparty, or utility procurement award) a PPA/capacity contract with contracted capacity >= 200 MW under one agreement/award. Otherwise NO.
evidence: TechCrunch reports Fervo has advanced past the 'valley of death,' indicating improving bankability and scale-up prospects.
monitoring_signals: Utility RFP awards and integrated resource plan (IRP) approvals
Fervo financing announcements and drilling/flow-rate updates
Data-center/utility offtake deals tied to firm clean power
predictions.rationale: Scaling geothermal to a single 200MW contract is feasible but depends on drilling performance, interconnection, and counterparty procurement timelines.

predictions.text: Reuters, Bloomberg, or FT will report that marine war-risk premiums (or equivalent additional insurance costs) for transiting the Strait of Hormuz increased by at least 50% versus the February 2026 average, citing insurers/brokers.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolved YES if at least one of Reuters/Bloomberg/FT publishes a report explicitly quantifying that Hormuz transit war-risk premiums (or comparable add-on insurance cost) are up >=50% vs the Feb 2026 average (or 'last month' where 'last month' is Feb 2026). Otherwise NO.
evidence: Ongoing U.S.-Israel-Iran tensions elevate perceived risk to regional energy infrastructure and shipping lanes.
monitoring_signals: Insurer/broker market notes (Lloyd’s market, Marsh, Aon) quoted in major media
Shipping rerouting or convoy/escort announcements
Documented incidents near Hormuz affecting commercial traffic
predictions.rationale: Insurance repricing is a common early-market response to conflict risk; threshold requires a clearly quantified and reported jump.

predictions.text: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) will disclose in an SEC filing (10-Q, 10-K, or 8-K) that it received a subpoena, search warrant, or formal notice of investigation from a U.S. government authority relating to export controls/sanctions compliance involving AI GPUs or servers.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolved YES if an SMCI SEC filing explicitly states receipt of a subpoena/search warrant/formal investigation notice from DOJ/BIS/OFAC/HSI/FBI (or similar) tied to export-control/sanctions compliance regarding AI GPUs/servers. Media-only reporting without SEC disclosure does not qualify. Otherwise NO.
evidence: CNBC reports prosecutors allege SMCI employees diverted Nvidia chips to China, increasing odds of formal investigative steps that require disclosure.
monitoring_signals: SMCI SEC filings and risk-factor/legal-proceedings updates
DOJ/BIS enforcement announcements naming corporate subjects
Auditor commentary and internal-control disclosures
predictions.rationale: Even with public allegations, SEC-disclosed investigative notices are not guaranteed (timing/materiality), making the near-term filing disclosure uncertain.
